hub.daily_challenge_desc: Answer 5 questions, beat your streak and climb the global leaderboard today!

4_3_3.desc: One of the most popular formations in world football. Ideal for teams with quick wide forwards and a technically gifted central striker.
4_3_3.str0: High pressing with three forwards pins the opposition's defense
4_3_3.str1: Wide attack stretches the opposition and creates spaces
4_3_3.str2: Central trio controls tempo and transitions
4_3_3.weak0: Vulnerable through the center when midfield loses the ball
4_3_3.weak1: Full-backs must cover huge distances to support wide areas
4_3_3.weak2: Requires high-intensity pressing from all three forwards
4_3_3.tip0: Central CM acts as an interior, constantly attacking and recovering
4_3_3.tip1: Wide forwards cut inside, freeing space for overlapping full-backs
4_3_3.tip2: Press trigger: striker presses the CB, wide forwards close wing-backs
4_3_3.tip3: Without the ball, shift to 4-5-1 — wide forwards drop into midfield
4_3_3.roles: Wide forwards are the team's attacking force. Central CM is the brain — covers the most ground.

4_4_2.desc: Classic flat-4 formation with two banks of four. Solid and easy to understand at any level. Effective with disciplined play and a strong striking pair.
4_4_2.str0: Compact defensive shape with two banks of four players
4_4_2.str1: Wide midfielders provide attacking width and track back
4_4_2.str2: Two strikers create constant threat and combine together
4_4_2.str3: Simple shape that is easy to explain to players
4_4_2.weak0: Vulnerable in the center against a three-man midfield
4_4_2.weak1: Wide midfielders must cover huge distances and can tire
4_4_2.weak2: Limited creativity in the middle with only two central midfielders
4_4_2.tip0: Wide midfielders are two-way players: support attacks and track back
4_4_2.tip1: Striking partnership is about combination: one holds, one makes runs
4_4_2.tip2: CMs aggressively close the center — no free passes through the middle
4_4_2.tip3: At set-pieces both strikers and at least one CM attack the box
4_4_2.roles: Striking pair — link-up and understanding are critical. CMs — high work-rate and interceptions.

4_2_3_1.desc: Tactically flexible formation. A double pivot protects the backline while an attacking trio and an AM create around a lone striker. Used by elite clubs worldwide.
4_2_3_1.str0: Double pivot provides a reliable shield in front of the defense
4_2_3_1.str1: AM links midfield and attack with creative freedom
4_2_3_1.str2: Attacking three offers numerous combination options
4_2_3_1.str3: Excellent for ball control and dictating the tempo of the game
4_2_3_1.weak0: Striker can become isolated without quick support
4_2_3_1.weak1: Wide areas exposed when wide midfielders bomb forward
4_2_3_1.weak2: Requires a technically and tactically strong AM
4_2_3_1.tip0: AM constantly moves between the lines to receive and turn
4_2_3_1.tip1: One pivot is the anchor, the second can advance with cover
4_2_3_1.tip2: Wide players attack, full-backs maintain position
4_2_3_1.tip3: Striker holds and combines with AM; AM makes late runs
4_2_3_1.roles: AM is the creative hub. The double pivot is the defensive spine of the team.

3_5_2.desc: Three centre-backs with wing-backs who act as both defenders and attackers. The five-man midfield creates numerical advantage in the centre.
3_5_2.str1: Wing-backs provide width in attack without sacrificing defense
3_5_2.str2: Three CBs comfortably deal with crosses and pressure
3_5_2.str3: Flexible: easily transitions to 5-3-2 when possession is lost
3_5_2.weak0: Space behind the wing-backs is the primary vulnerability
3_5_2.weak1: Wing-backs require exceptional fitness and technical ability
3_5_2.weak2: Three CBs must be comfortable playing out from the back
3_5_2.tip0: Wing-backs are key players: versatile in both phases
3_5_2.tip1: When possession is lost, wing-backs immediately drop into 5-3-2
3_5_2.tip2: Central CB always organises and commands the back three
3_5_2.tip3: High press initiated by the two strikers at opposition goal-kicks
3_5_2.roles: Wing-backs are key players. Central CB organises. Central trio must be tireless.

4_1_4_1.desc: Structured formation with a single pivot as a shield between the defence and a four-strong midfield. The lone striker leads counter-attacking play.
4_1_4_1.str0: Single pivot covers the defence from deep
4_1_4_1.str1: Four midfielders dominate central and wide zones
4_1_4_1.str2: Wide players can attack with the pivot providing cover behind them
4_1_4_1.str3: Compact block is very difficult to break down
4_1_4_1.weak0: Pivot can be overloaded against two strikers
4_1_4_1.weak1: Lone striker must hold the ball and press simultaneously
4_1_4_1.weak2: Too passive if wide midfielders do not push forward
4_1_4_1.tip0: The pivot must have elite positional awareness and game intelligence
4_1_4_1.tip1: Wide midfielders provide width and crosses — the main attacking outlet
4_1_4_1.tip2: Striker presses the CB and holds the ball with back to goal
4_1_4_1.tip3: In attack, pivot distributes and wide players make runs in behind
4_1_4_1.roles: Pivot is the cornerstone of the defensive structure. Wide midfielders are the main attacking threat.

3_4_3.desc: Aggressive formation with three CBs and four midfielders supporting three forwards. Popularised by Conte. Requires athletic and disciplined players.
3_4_3.str0: Three forwards apply constant pressure on the opposition defense
3_4_3.str1: Four midfielders control central zones with possession
3_4_3.str2: Three CBs outnumber opponents with crosses and headers
3_4_3.str3: Midfield box wins second balls and transitions quickly to attack
3_4_3.weak0: Space behind the wing-backs can be exploited by the opposition
3_4_3.weak1: Back three must be comfortable with the ball
3_4_3.weak2: Wing-backs cover enormous distances in both directions
3_4_3.tip0: Wing-backs are the widest players in the team when in possession
3_4_3.tip1: Back three hold a flat line with the central CB constantly organising
3_4_3.tip2: Three forwards press as a unit
3_4_3.tip3: Without the ball, wing-backs quickly drop into a 5-4-1 block
3_4_3.roles: Wing-backs: attack and defend. Central CB organises the back three.

4_3_2_1.desc: Known as the Christmas Tree. Three CMs supply two attacking midfielders behind a lone striker. Creates overloads in central zones.
4_3_2_1.str0: Overloads central zones at every level of the pitch
4_3_2_1.str1: Two AMs find pockets between the opposition's lines
4_3_2_1.str2: High creativity around the striker from multiple angles
4_3_2_1.str3: Strong defensive structure with four disciplined defenders
4_3_2_1.weak0: No natural width — full-backs provide all the width
4_3_2_1.weak1: Both AMs require high technique and positional intelligence
4_3_2_1.weak2: Vulnerable to being overloaded on the flanks by wide formations
4_3_2_1.tip0: Full-backs constantly push forward to provide width
4_3_2_1.tip1: AMs position between the lines to receive the ball on the half-turn
4_3_2_1.tip2: Striker holds, links with the AMs and makes runs
4_3_2_1.tip3: When the ball is switched the entire block moves horizontally quickly
4_3_2_1.roles: Two AMs are the creative engine. Full-backs provide all the width.

4_5_1.desc: Highly defensive formation that packs the midfield. Designed to absorb pressure and hit on the counter. The striker must be quick, composed, and tireless.
4_5_1.str0: Five midfielders make the centre virtually impassable
4_5_1.str1: Compact 4-5 block is very difficult to break down
4_5_1.str2: Lone striker is dangerous in quick counter-attacks
4_5_1.str3: Ideal for protecting a lead or facing a superior opponent
4_5_1.weak0: Very limited in attack — the priority is winning the ball back
4_5_1.weak1: Lone striker is isolated and receives minimal support
4_5_1.weak2: Wide midfielders carry an enormous defensive workload
4_5_1.tip0: Striker must be the fastest player — built for counter-attacks
4_5_1.tip1: The five press in waves — never chase alone
4_5_1.tip2: Wide midfielders drop immediately with every loss of possession
4_5_1.tip3: Win the ball — find the striker quickly with a direct pass
4_5_1.roles: Striker is the composed finisher on the counter. Wide midfielders are tireless workers.

5_3_2.desc: Defensive formation based on five defenders. Wing-backs provide width in attack while the back five shuts the opposition out. Two strikers are dangerous on the counter.
5_3_2.str0: Maximum defensive solidity with five players at the back
5_3_2.str1: Covers all central and wide zones
5_3_2.str2: Wing-backs give attacking width without high risk
5_3_2.str3: Two strikers combine well on counter-attacks
5_3_2.weak0: Limited attacking threat — relies on counter-attacks
5_3_2.weak2: Requires high tactical discipline from everyone
5_3_2.tip0: Use when protecting a lead or facing a superior opponent
5_3_2.tip1: Strikers must be quick — the 2v2 counter is the main weapon
5_3_2.tip2: The back five hold a strict line — no one breaks out of the structure
5_3_2.tip3: CMs cover space between the lines — they do not advance
5_3_2.roles: Central CB organises the back five. Strikers are fast finishers on the counter.

3_3_2.desc: The most common 9v9 formation. A familiar and balanced structure with clear roles for all players.
3_3_2.str0: Compact and simple for young players to understand
3_3_2.str1: Central three dominate the middle zones
3_3_2.str2: Two strikers create constant threat and combine together
3_3_2.weak0: Limited width — difficult to use the flanks effectively
3_3_2.weak1: Central CM overloaded as the link player
3_3_2.tip0: Wide CMs take wider positions to provide attacking width
3_3_2.tip1: Strikers stay close together — work as a pair
3_3_2.tip2: On loss of possession quickly form a compact 3-3 block
3_3_2.roles: Central CM is the organiser and link player. Strikers must understand each other.

3_2_3.desc: Attacking 9v9 formation with three forwards and only two midfielders. Best for technically superior teams looking to dominate possession.
3_2_3.str0: Three forwards create constant pressure on the opponent's defence
3_2_3.str1: Wide attackers stretch the opposition and create space
3_2_3.str2: Quick combinations between forwards and midfielders
3_2_3.weak0: Only two CMs — the middle opens up when the press fails
3_2_3.weak1: Requires very high fitness from both midfielders
3_2_3.tip0: CMs work as a pair: one attacks, the other covers
3_2_3.tip1: Wide forwards drop back when possession is lost
3_2_3.tip2: Back three holds a strict compact line
3_2_3.roles: Wide striking pair is the main attacking weapon. The two CMs share all the work.

4_3_1.desc: Structured defensive 9v9 formation with maximum coverage in midfield. The lone striker leads counter-attacking play.
4_3_1.str0: Four defenders form a solid and compact line
4_3_1.str1: Midfield trio effectively covers central zones
4_3_1.str2: Lone striker is dangerous on counter-attacks into open space
4_3_1.weak0: Single striker receives almost no support
4_3_1.weak1: Cedes possession and territory to the opposition
4_3_1.tip0: Striker must be fast and capable of holding the ball
4_3_1.tip1: CMs can take turns pushing forward to provide support
4_3_1.tip2: Use when needing to protect a result
4_3_1.roles: Striker is all-round: pressing, holding, finishing. CMs — work-rate above all.

2_4_2.desc: Attacking 9v9 formation with four midfielders and two strikers but only two defenders. For technically superior teams.
2_4_2.str0: Four midfielders dominate possession and create overloads
2_4_2.str1: Two strikers apply constant pressure on the defenders
2_4_2.str2: Wide midfielders provide width and goal threat
2_4_2.weak0: Two defenders are under high pressure and vulnerable
2_4_2.weak1: Midfielders must immediately drop when possession is lost
2_4_2.weak2: High risk — any structural breakdown leads to a goal
2_4_2.tip0: Both CBs confident in duels and in the air
2_4_2.tip1: Wide midfielders defend the flanks when CBs are under pressure
2_4_2.tip2: One CM always stays closer to the defensive line
2_4_2.tip3: Use only when the team has technical dominance
2_4_2.roles: Both CBs must be the best defenders. One CM must stay behind.

3_4_1.desc: Structured 9v9 formation with three defenders, four midfielders and one striker. Midfield dominance is the key characteristic.
3_4_1.str2: Wide midfielders give attacking width in attack and defence
3_4_1.weak0: Lone striker must work very hard
3_4_1.tip0: Central midfielders set the tempo and control possession
3_4_1.tip1: Wide players deliver crosses and get back into position
3_4_1.tip2: Striker presses CBs high and holds up play
3_4_1.tip3: Back three stays compact — no one steps out to chase the ball
3_4_1.roles: Central midfielders are the engine. Striker is all-round.

4_2_2.desc: Solid defensive 9v9 formation with a strong four and two central midfielders. Two strikers provide a direct counter-attacking threat.
4_2_2.str0: Back four creates an organised defensive line
4_2_2.str1: Two strikers are a constant counter-attacking threat
4_2_2.weak0: Two CMs are heavily overloaded in the middle
4_2_2.weak1: Midfield is easily bypassed by the opposition
4_2_2.weak2: Limited options for building out from the back
4_2_2.tip0: Two CMs — elite work-rate, do everything in defence and attack
4_2_2.tip1: Strikers as a pair: one holds, the other makes runs
4_2_2.tip3: Win possession — attack immediately, delay kills the counter
4_2_2.roles: Two CMs carry all the midfield burden. Striking pair must combine well.

2_3_3.desc: High-risk, high-reward attacking 9v9 formation. Three forwards and three midfielders create supremacy in the final third with only two defenders.
2_3_3.str0: Three forwards create relentless pressure on the opponent
2_3_3.str1: Midfield trio supports attacks while maintaining shape
2_3_3.str2: Numerical superiority in the final third creates many chances
2_3_3.weak0: Two defenders are extremely vulnerable to counter-attacks
2_3_3.weak1: Midfielders must drop immediately when possession is lost
2_3_3.weak2: High risk when pressed — 3v2s appear quickly
2_3_3.tip0: Both CBs must be the best individual defenders
2_3_3.tip1: One midfielder always stays behind to provide cover
2_3_3.tip2: Wide forwards press the opponent's CBs high and aggressively
2_3_3.tip3: Use only when the team has technical and physical dominance
2_3_3.roles: Both CBs — elite in duels. 'Holding CM' — critical cover.

2_2_1.desc: The most common 6v6 formation. Two defensive blocks with one striker — simple and balanced structure.
2_2_1.str0: Simple structure that is easy to explain and organise
2_2_1.str1: Equal numbers in defence and midfield
2_2_1.str2: Striker is always available for counter-attacks
2_2_1.weak0: No natural width — all play goes through the centre
2_2_1.tip0: Both CMs actively and frequently support the striker
2_2_1.tip1: Defenders hold their line — they do not step out to chase
2_2_1.tip2: At set-pieces one CM joins the striker
2_2_1.roles: CMs link all lines. Striker must be sharp and composed.

1_3_1.desc: Attacking 6v6 formation with three midfielders and a single defender. Midfield dominance is the focus. Only for teams with a strong defensive organiser.
1_3_1.str0: Three midfielders control and dominate possession
1_3_1.str2: Lone striker regularly receives the ball in good positions
1_3_1.weak0: Single CB carries enormous pressure and responsibility
1_3_1.weak1: If midfielders do not drop back the CB is in a 2v1
1_3_1.tip0: CB must be the best defender: composed, dominant, and experienced
1_3_1.tip1: Wide midfielders drop to help the CB when needed
1_3_1.tip2: Use against weaker opponents to control the game
1_3_1.roles: CB is the most important player — the entire defence depends on them.

2_1_2.desc: Wide attacking 6v6 formation with two wide attackers and a single CM as the defensive anchor.
2_1_2.str0: Maximum width through two wide forwards
2_1_2.str1: Quick wide attacks create crossing opportunities
2_1_2.str2: CM provides defensive link with the two defenders
2_1_2.weak0: Centre opens up when the wide players push forward
2_1_2.weak1: Single CM cannot cope with central overloads
2_1_2.tip0: Wide players are the main weapon: quick and able to take players on 1v1
2_1_2.tip1: CM is disciplined — does not stray far from the defensive line
2_1_2.tip2: Defenders push forward when the wide players cover behind them
2_1_2.roles: Wide attackers are the main attacking force. CM is the disciplined defensive anchor.

2_1_1.desc: Classic 5v5 structure: two defenders, one midfielder and one striker. Simple, balanced and effective.
2_1_1.str0: Clear and simple structure for all players
2_1_1.str1: Two defenders provide solid cover in the defensive zone
2_1_1.str2: CM naturally links defence and attack
2_1_1.weak0: Limited attacking options with only one striker
2_1_1.weak1: CM overloaded — defends and attacks at the same time
2_1_1.tip0: Striker positions between the two opposition defenders
2_1_1.tip1: CM is the key player: two-way, covers all areas
2_1_1.tip2: Both CBs hold a compact central zone
2_1_1.roles: CM is the key player — covers everything. Striker must be sharp.

1_2_1.desc: Attacking 5v5 formation with two midfielders creating central superiority. The lone defender carries huge responsibility.
1_2_1.str0: Two CMs create central superiority and control possession
1_2_1.str1: Multiple passing options between the lines
1_2_1.str2: Striker regularly receives the ball in dangerous positions
1_2_1.weak1: CMs must drop immediately when possession is lost
1_2_1.tip0: CB must be the most composed and experienced defender — mistakes are punished
1_2_1.tip1: CMs rotate: one attacks, the other covers the CB
1_2_1.tip2: Striker stays close to goal and acts as the finisher
1_2_1.roles: CB is an elite duelist. Two CMs share defensive and creative responsibilities.

3_1.desc: Ultra-defensive 5v5 formation with three defenders and one striker. Only for protecting a lead or containing a stronger opponent.
3_1.str0: Three defenders form a virtually impenetrable block
3_1.str1: Covers all central and wide zones
3_1.str2: Lone striker is a direct counter-attacking weapon
3_1.weak0: Almost no attacking presence — entirely reactive
3_1.weak1: Striker is completely isolated without support
3_1.tip0: Use only when protecting a lead or needing a draw
3_1.tip1: Striker must be quick — the counter is the only weapon
3_1.tip2: Back three holds a strict flat line — no one steps out
3_1.roles: Striker is the fast counter-attacking finisher. Three CBs maintain structure and communicate.

league_details.no_tournaments_in_league: There are no tournaments in this league yet
